summary: Ukrainian Neptune missile struck a Russian Black Sea Fleet target in Sevastopol, destroying ammunition and equipment storage. Intensive UAV activity was reported across multiple front‑line oblasts, including attacks on logistics near Armянськ and rail infrastructure in Sumy. Russian tactical aviation conducted guided bomb strikes on Kharkiv, Donetsk and other regions, while Ukraine reported a shortfall in air‑defence missiles reducing ballistic missile interception rates.
key_points: Ukrainian Neptune missile hit a Russian Black Sea Fleet target in Sevastopol, destroying ammunition and military equipment storage (Source: @UkraineNow/81849).
Ukrainian forces struck a convoy of up to 50 Russian trucks near the bridge at Armянськ, disabling the bridge and halting a key logistics route (Source: @UkraineNow/81850).
Russian tactical aviation launched guided aviation bomb attacks on Kharkiv, Donetsk and other regions, targeting settlements and infrastructure (Source: @kpszsu/64897, @kpszsu/64916, @kpszsu/64874).
Ukrainian air‑defence down‑rate for ballistic missiles has fallen due to a shortage of missiles, according to the Ukrainian Air Force spokesman (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30847).

Massive night attack on a railway station in Sumy region killed a 44‑year‑old woman and injured another; a separate UAV strike in Mykolaiv caused three casualties and a large fire (Source: @UkraineNow/81854).
Ukrainian forces reported multiple UAV incursions across Sumy, Kharkiv, Dnipropetrovsk, Odesa and other oblasts, with varied flight courses (Source: @kpszsu/64907, @kpszsu/64898, @eRadarrua/96298).
Russian police detained a knife‑wielding foreigner in Naro‑Fominsk, resulting in one death and one injury (Source: @tass_agency/380353).
High probability of a medium‑range ballistic missile launch from the Kapustin Yar test site was warned for the day (Source: @kpszsu/64925).

sections.executive_summary: Ukrainian Neptune missile struck a Russian Black Sea Fleet target in Sevastopol, destroying ammunition and equipment storage. Ukrainian forces hit a large Russian logistics convoy near Armянськ, disabling a key bridge and halting supply movements. Russian tactical aviation continued guided bomb strikes across Kharkiv, Donetsk and other front‑line areas while Ukraine faces a shortage of air‑defence missiles, reducing ballistic missile interception rates.

points: Ukrainian Neptune cruise missile hit a Russian Black Sea Fleet facility in Sevastopol, destroying ammunition and military equipment storage (Source: @UkraineNow/81849).
Ukrainian strike on a convoy of up to 50 Russian trucks near the bridge at Armянськ disabled the bridge and halted a major logistics corridor (Source: @UkraineNow/81850).

Ukrainian forces attacked a railway station in Shostkin community, Sumy region, killing a 44‑year‑old woman and injuring another; a separate UAV strike in Mykolaiv caused three casualties and a large fire (Source: @UkraineNow/81854).
Ukrainian air‑defence down‑rate for ballistic missiles has decreased due to a shortage of missiles, according to a Ukrainian Air Force spokesman (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30847).

Multiple UAV sightings reported across Sumy, Kharkiv, Dnipropetrovsk, Odesa and other oblasts, with courses ranging from north‑west to south‑west, indicating sustained aerial reconnaissance and strike activity (Source: @kpszsu/64907, @kpszsu/64898, @eRadarrua/96298).
Ukrainian forces reported a successful fire‑suppression operation after a UAV‑induced blaze at a power plant area in Simferopol, Crimea (Source: @eRadarrua/96289).
Ukrainian authorities warned of a high probability of a medium‑range ballistic missile launch from Kapustin Yar, urging vigilance to air‑raid alerts (Source: @kpszsu/64925).
Russian tactical aviation launched guided aviation bomb attacks on Kharkiv region, targeting settlements and infrastructure (Source: @kpszsu/64897).
Guided bomb strikes were also reported in Donetsk region by Russian tactical aviation (Source: @kpszsu/64916).
Russian forces conducted guided bomb attacks on Sumy region, employing aviation‑guided munitions (Source: @kpszsu/64892).
Russian police detained a foreign attacker with a knife in Naro‑Fominsk, resulting in one fatality and one injury (Source: @tass_agency/380353).
Russian air‑defence reportedly intercepted 102 hostile UAVs during the night of 11‑12 June, including Shahed, Gerbera, Italmas and imitation drones from multiple directions (Source: @kpszsu/64903).
Russian forces reported a night‑time rail station attack in Sumy region, causing civilian casualties (Source: @UkraineNow/81854).
Russian tactical aviation activity was noted on the southern direction, indicating potential upcoming strikes (Source: @kpszsu/64922).
Russian missile launch activity from Kapustin Yar was warned as a high‑probability event for the day (Source: @kpszsu/64925).
Ukrainian guided bomb attacks were reported in Kharkiv region, with UAVs observed over Synelnykivka district heading north‑west (Source: @kpszsu/64897).
Russian tactical aviation launched guided bomb strikes on Kharkiv, targeting multiple settlements (Source: @kpszsu/64897).
Ukrainian UAVs were sighted over the Luhansk‑adjacent area near Novomykolaivka, heading west (Source: @kpszsu/64897).
Ukrainian forces reported UAV activity over the Bilhorod‑Dnistrovskyi district of Odesa, moving westward (Source: @kpszsu/64897).
Russian guided bomb attacks were also noted in Donetsk region, indicating continued pressure on the eastern front (Source: @kpszsu/64916).
Ukrainian UAVs reported near Zolochiv and Bohodukhiv in Kharkiv region, moving south (Source: @kpszsu/64909).
Ukrainian air‑defence reported a decrease in ballistic missile interception due to missile shortages, affecting eastern sector protection (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30847).
High probability warning for a medium‑range ballistic missile launch from Kapustin Yar, potentially targeting eastern front areas (Source: @kpszsu/64925).
Ukrainian UAVs reported in Chernihiv region, including over Horodnia/Snovsk from the north (Source: @eRadarrua/96300).
Ukrainian UAVs observed near Semenivka in Chernihiv region (Source: @eRadarrua/96284).
Ukrainian UAVs spotted in northern Chernihiv heading south‑west towards Duboviazivka (Source: @kpszsu/64907).
Russian tactical aviation activity warned on the north‑east direction, suggesting possible strikes (Source: @kpszsu/64912).
Ukrainian UAVs reported near the border area of Sumy heading north towards Konotop (Source: @eRadarrua/96298).
Ukrainian forces noted UAVs over the northern part of Kharkiv region near Zolochiv (Source: @kpszsu/64909).
Air‑raid alerts issued for potential ballistic missile threats from the south, affecting northern oblasts (Source: @eRadarrua/96297).
Ukrainian air‑defence reported a shortfall in missiles, impacting interception capability across the northern front (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30847).
Ukrainian UAVs reported over Mykolaiv region heading north, indicating surveillance or strike missions (Source: @kpszsu/64898).
Ukrainian UAVs observed in southern Kharkiv region near Lozova (Source: @kpszsu/64882).
Ukrainian forces struck a Russian logistics convoy near Armянськ, disabling a bridge and halting fuel and ammunition shipments (Source: @UkraineNow/81850).
Ukrainian UAV attack on a railway station in Sumy region caused civilian casualties (Source: @UkraineNow/81854).
Russian tactical aviation activity noted on the southern direction, suggesting possible upcoming strikes (Source: @kpszsu/64922).
Ukrainian forces reported a fire at a power plant area in Simferopol, Crimea after a UAV strike (Source: @eRadarrua/96289).
High probability warning for a medium‑range ballistic missile launch from Kapustin Yar, potentially affecting southern oblasts (Source: @kpszsu/64925).
Ukrainian air‑defence shortage of missiles reduces ballistic missile interception, impacting southern sector protection (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30847).
Ukrainian Neptune cruise missile successfully hit a Russian Black Sea Fleet target in Sevastopol, destroying ammunition and equipment (Source: @UkraineNow/81849).
Russian tactical aviation launched guided aviation bomb attacks on Kharkiv, Donetsk and Sumy regions, using precision munitions (Source: @kpszsu/64897, @kpszsu/64916, @kpszsu/64892).
Ukrainian forces reported extensive UAV activity across multiple oblasts, with courses indicating reconnaissance and strike roles (Source: @kpszsu/64907, @kpszsu/64898, @eRadarrua/96298).
Russian air‑defence claimed interception of 102 hostile UAVs during the night of 11‑12 June, including Shahed, Gerbera, Italmas and imitation drones (Source: @kpszsu/64903).
Ukrainian air‑defence down‑rate for ballistic missiles has decreased due to missile shortages (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30847).
High probability warning issued for a medium‑range ballistic missile launch from Kapustin Yar, prompting heightened alertness (Source: @kpszsu/64925).
Ukrainian UAVs observed over the Black Sea coast and Crimea, including a fire at a power plant area in Simferopol (Source: @eRadarrua/96289).
Russian tactical aviation activity noted on the southern and north‑east directions, indicating potential multi‑axis air operations (Source: @kpszsu/64922, @kpszsu/64912).

key_developments: Ukrainian Neptune missile struck a Russian Black Sea Fleet target in Sevastopol, destroying ammunition and equipment storage (Source: @UkraineNow/81849).
Ukrainian strike on a convoy of up to 50 Russian trucks near the bridge at Armянськ disabled the bridge and halted a key logistics route (Source: @UkraineNow/81850).
Russian tactical aviation launched guided bomb attacks on Kharkiv, Donetsk and Sumy regions, targeting settlements and infrastructure (Source: @kpszsu/64897, @kpszsu/64916, @kpszsu/64892).
Ukrainian air‑defence down‑rate for ballistic missiles fell due to a shortage of missiles, reducing interception capability (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30847).
Massive night attack on a railway station in Sumy region killed a civilian and injured another; a UAV strike in Mykolaiv caused three casualties and a large fire (Source: @UkraineNow/81854).
Russian air‑defence reported intercepting 102 hostile UAVs during the night of 11‑12 June, including Shahed and imitation drones (Source: @kpszsu/64903).
High probability warning for a medium‑range ballistic missile launch from Kapustin Yar was issued for the day (Source: @kpszsu/64925).
sections.threat_assessment: Russian tactical aviation continues to employ guided aviation bombs across the eastern and southern fronts, targeting both civilian infrastructure and military logistics. Ukraine's recent Neptune strike demonstrates its growing long‑range strike capability, but the shortage of air‑defence missiles hampers its ability to counter Russian ballistic missile launches, increasing vulnerability on all fronts. The high probability of a medium‑range ballistic missile launch from Kapustin Yar adds a significant threat of sudden, high‑impact strikes, especially against densely populated or strategic sites.
sections.regional_response: Ukrainian command has intensified UAV reconnaissance and strike missions across multiple oblasts, focusing on disrupting Russian supply lines such as the convoy near Armянськ. Local authorities in affected regions, including Sumy and Mykolaiv, have activated emergency services and reinforced civil defence measures following civilian casualties. Russian forces have increased air‑defence activity, claiming high interception numbers, while also maintaining pressure through guided bomb attacks on front‑line settlements.
sections.intelligence_notes: Continued monitoring of UAV flight paths reveals coordinated patterns aimed at both reconnaissance and strike roles, suggesting a broader Ukrainian aerial strategy. The depletion of Ukrainian air‑defence missiles may force a shift to alternative counter‑measures, such as mobile air‑defence units or increased reliance on electronic warfare. Russian tactical aviation's persistent use of guided bombs indicates a focus on degrading Ukrainian command, control and logistics infrastructure, warranting heightened alertness in the eastern and southern sectors.
